brief_name: Systematic - The Living Brief for Systematic Traders
lead_signal: Volatility is elevated, but Systematic should wait for cross-asset confirmation before treating this as a regime break.
source_integrity: This sample uses imported operating docs and public-source placeholders. Market values are illustrative until live ingestion is wired.

regime_watch.title: VIX remains elevated while credit spreads are not yet confirming stress
regime_watch.source: CBOE VIX and FRED ICE BofA spread series
regime_watch.evidence: Volatility is elevated, but high-yield spreads remain below the stress trigger defined in the operating docs.
regime_watch.summary: The tape is uncomfortable, not broken. Volatility is warning that positioning is fragile, while credit has not yet moved into a broad risk-off regime.
regime_watch.systematic_signal: Trend systems should treat this as regime friction, not as permission to override live rules.

arbitrage_moat.future_vector: If HY OAS widens through the watch threshold, this shifts from noisy volatility to confirmed credit stress.
arbitrage_moat.arbitrage: Many readers will overreact to VIX alone; the edge is waiting for cross-asset confirmation.
arbitrage_moat.moat_move: Track when volatility-only alerts resolve or fail so Systematic builds a source accuracy record.

regime_watch.caveat: VIX can spike around event risk and options positioning without becoming a durable trend regime change.
regime_watch.regime_read: HOT: risk-on momentum with elevated volatility and partial confirmation only.

archive_memory.then: VIX spike faded after three sessions without credit confirmation.
archive_memory.lesson: Do not treat volatility alone as a system change.

strategy_performance_signals.title: Crowded equity futures positioning raises mean-reversion risk
strategy_performance_signals.evidence: Non-commercial ES positioning is described as crowded long in the sample operating model.
strategy_performance_signals.summary: Crowding is not a sell signal by itself, but it makes trend continuation more fragile. A reversal in next week's COT report would matter more than today's crowding headline.
strategy_performance_signals.systematic_signal: Positioning risk belongs in the watchlist and risk review, not in discretionary prediction.

arbitrage_moat.future_vector: The actionable signal is a week-over-week reversal in positioning, not the existence of crowding alone.
arbitrage_moat.arbitrage: Most commentary treats crowded positioning as immediate direction; Systematic can frame it as conditional risk.
arbitrage_moat.moat_move: Build a COT archive that records whether crowded readings actually preceded systematic drawdowns.

strategy_performance_signals.caveat: COT data is delayed and should be cross-checked with price, volatility, and liquidity.

archive_memory.then: Crowding preceded a short equity correction, but trend rules recovered without parameter changes.
archive_memory.lesson: Crowding informs heat, not trade direction.

research_edge.title: The useful research question is robustness, not fresh alpha
research_edge.evidence: The operating docs prioritize signal design, out-of-sample skepticism, transaction costs, and implementation drag.
research_edge.summary: The brief should not chase every new backtest. It should filter research through whether it changes robustness, execution, sizing, or regime classification.
research_edge.systematic_signal: Research belongs in the brief only when it can change a system design assumption or an operational checklist.

arbitrage_moat.future_vector: The research section should mature into a recurring implementation-quality score.
arbitrage_moat.arbitrage: There is more reader value in rejecting weak research than summarizing lots of papers.
arbitrage_moat.moat_move: Create a recurring archive of rejected research patterns and why they failed the Systematic filter.

research_edge.caveat: This is an internal operating principle, not an external market observation.

regulatory_structure.title: Data licensing is a product risk, not just an engineering detail
regulatory_structure.evidence: The source strategy distinguishes public-domain sources from proprietary data that may require attribution or limit republication.
regulatory_structure.summary: The MVP should lean on public and clearly citable sources. Premium sources can improve speed, but they also increase licensing, attribution, and cost obligations.
regulatory_structure.systematic_signal: A source-grounded brief is only defensible if its data rights and citation rules are operationally clean.

arbitrage_moat.future_vector: Before paid launch, source rights should become a checklist item in the publication workflow.
arbitrage_moat.arbitrage: A transparent source-rights posture can become trust infrastructure while competitors stay vague.
arbitrage_moat.moat_move: Track source licensing status alongside source tier and source accuracy.

regulatory_structure.caveat: Specific vendor terms still need direct legal/product review before commercial use.
regulatory_structure.risk: Republishing proprietary data or news summaries too aggressively could create licensing exposure.

cta_competitor_watch.title: The near-term moat is a disciplined archive, not a faster news feed
cta_competitor_watch.source: Living Briefs north star and GStack review
cta_competitor_watch.evidence: The north star defines archive memory, source reputation, and constrained conversation as the product advantage.
cta_competitor_watch.summary: Systematic should not compete with Bloomberg or Reuters on speed. It should compound memory around source reliability, repeated regimes, caveats, and decisions avoided.
cta_competitor_watch.systematic_signal: The archive is the product surface that turns daily publishing into accumulated edge.

arbitrage_moat.future_vector: After 30 daily issues, comparison questions become a real feature; after 100, they become a moat.
arbitrage_moat.arbitrage: Most newsletters discard yesterday. Systematic should make yesterday queryable.
arbitrage_moat.moat_move: Store every finding, caveat, watch trigger, and recommended move as structured data from day one.

cta_competitor_watch.caveat: The moat only compounds if the brief is published consistently and outcomes are reviewed.

arbitrage_moat.near_term_arbitrage: Frame noisy market stress as conditional evidence instead of content urgency.
arbitrage_moat.future_signal: If the watchlist triggers confirm, the brief can update from HOT to STRESSED without sounding reactive.
arbitrage_moat.moat_strategy: Persist source tiers, caveats, archive memory, and outcome reviews for every finding.
arbitrage_moat.do_not_do: Do not turn VIX, COT, or CTA commentary into discretionary override language.

systematic_takes.take: The job is to reduce heat, not invent a prediction.
systematic_takes.why_it_matters: Crowding and volatility can make good systems feel broken before they actually are.
systematic_takes.action: Review drawdown bands, margin exposure, and rule adherence before touching parameters.

systematic_takes.take: Rejecting weak research is part of the edge.
systematic_takes.why_it_matters: Systematic traders are vulnerable to elegant backtests during uncomfortable regimes.
systematic_takes.action: Only promote research that changes a testable implementation assumption.

discipline_check: Do not override a live system because one volatility signal feels urgent.
hallucination_guardrails: Market values in this sample are illustrative.
COT positioning is delayed and should not be treated as real-time confirmation.
Internal docs are useful operating sources but not external market evidence.
No personalized financial advice is provided.
systematic_synthesis: The sample brief demonstrates the intended operating posture: source-grounded, conditional, archive-aware, and allergic to discretionary panic. The next build step is ingestion and storage, not more prose.
recommended_move: Create the first real archive entry and start tracking whether each watchlist trigger resolves, fails, or remains pending.

watchlist.why: Credit confirmation separates noisy volatility from broader market stress.
watchlist.trigger: HY OAS widens through the configured stress threshold or rises sharply over five sessions.

watchlist.item: COT reversal from crowded long positioning
watchlist.why: A positioning reversal matters more than crowded exposure alone.
watchlist.trigger: Next weekly COT report shows a material non-commercial reduction.

watchlist.why: The product promise depends on defensible citation and republication rules.
watchlist.trigger: Any paid vendor data is used in subscriber-facing output.

strategic_prompt: What will Systematic remember in 30 days that a normal market newsletter will have forgotten?
next_search: latest CFTC COT equity futures positioning managed futures trend following drawdown
